sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I need to map the remaining positions to understand the full grid
# Current green dot: (38-39, 8-9) = (top, left)
# Remaining: (middle, left), (middle, right), (bottom, center), (bottom, right)

# Let me map them all by moving the green dot and clicking on blue
# I'll track where the blue block goes for each position

positions = [
    ('middle_left', ['DOWN'], (40, 8)),
    ('middle_right', ['DOWN', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'], (40, 12)),
    ('bottom_center', ['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'RIGHT'], (42, 10)),
    ('bottom_right', ['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'], (42, 12)),
]

# First, reset green dot to (top, left) = (38-39, 8-9)
# It's already there

mapping = {}
for name, moves, target in positions:
    # Move green dot
    result = action(moves)
    # Click on blue shape
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 16:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            min_r, max_r = min(rows), max(rows)
            min_c, max_c = min(cols), max(cols)
            mapping[name] = f"[{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]"
            print(f"{name}: blue -> {mapping[name]}")
            break

print(f"\nFull mapping: {mapping}")

# Now let me check the current layout
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCurrent layout:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    min_r, max_r = min(rows), max(rows)
    min_c, max_c = min(cols), max(cols)
    if max_c < 30 and n['pixels'] > 4:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, "
              f"bbox=[{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
